title: RNA extraction from adult Aiptasia
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6oq3j","text":"A protocol for extracting RNA from Aiptasia, modified from the Weis lab protocol by Angela Poole and Mauricio Rodriguez-Lanetty. Note that the final product has significant DNA contamination and needs to be cleaned up via DNase treatment. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"98ngr","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"68t9b","text":"Make sure to setup a clean area in the fume hood, wipe down all pipettes with RNase Away/Zap, and cool down the centrifuge.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"588ri","text":"All Trizol steps should be performed in the hood. All Trizol waste should be treated as hazardous.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Homogenize starting material in Trizol using a micropestle. Trizol volume should be ~ 1 mL per 0.1g tissue. Once the homogenate is uniform, incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes. All Trizol steps (1-5) should be performed in a fume hood and all Trizol waste should be treated as hazardous.
Centrifuge samples for 10 minutes at 12,000 x g at 4°C. This step will pellet any debris and polysaccharides. Transfer the supernatant to a new tube and discard the pellet.
Add chloroform to the sample (0.2 mL per 1 mL of Trizol used) and shake vigorously for 20 seconds. Incubate samples at room temperature for 2-3 minutes.
Centrifuge samples for 18 minutes at 10,000 x g at 4°C. *Note: past this step you should be careful of RNase contamination*

Carefully remove the top aqueous (clear) layer and transfer to a new sterile, RNase-free tube. IMPORTANT: When you take your samples out of the centrifuge, you will see three layers: aqueous (clear, top), interphase (white, middle) and organic (red, bottom). Stay away from the white middle layer - that is where the DNases and RNases are. It is better to leave a little of the aqueous layer behind than to risk contamination.
Slowly add an equal volume of 100% RNase-free EtOH and mix gently by pipetting or inverting tube.

Load sample (up to 700 μL) into an RNeasy spin column (Qiagen) and spin at room temperature for 1 min at 8,000 x g. Discard flow through. If your total volume exceeds 700 μL, repeat this step until all of the sample has passed over the column.
Add 700 μl of Buffer RW1 to the column and spin at room temperature for 1 min at 8,000 x g. Discard flow through.
Transfer spin column to a new collection tube and add 500 μl of Buffer RPE (check that ethanol has been added) and spin at room temperature for 1 min at 8,000 x g. Discard flow through.
Add 500 μl of Buffer RPE and spin at room temperature for 2 min at 8,000 x g. Discard flow through.
Spin for an additional 1 minute at 8,000 x g to get rid of remaining buffer in column.

Transfer the spin column to a new, RNase-free 1.5 mL collection tube and pipette 30-50 μl of RNase-free water over the center of the membrane (do not touch the membrane with your pipette tip!). Let the sample sit at room temperature for 1 minute and then spin for 1 minute at 8,000 x g to elute RNA.
Discard the spin column and store your RNA at -80°C until further use.
authors: modified from Weis lab protocol by Mauricio Rodriguez-Lanetty
creator: Kelsey Speer
